key_insight: The paper replaces crude diagonal mass-matrix inverses with a broken-space additive Schwarz (BRAS) approximation that is still local and sparse, but much closer to the true inverse.
engineering_relevance: BRAS can speed up explicit time integration and block preconditioners for finite-element systems, especially for higher-order elements where diagonal/Jacobi mass approximations are weak.
method: Apply exact element-local inverse mass matrices on the broken finite-element space.
Average the broken-space result back to the conforming space to form a symmetric positive definite approximation.
Reuse standard mass-assembly data structures: element matrices, local-to-global maps, and element-adjacency sparsity.
evidence_signal: Across H1, H(curl), and H(div) elements on 2D/3D simplicial meshes with polynomial orders p=1 to 4, BRAS reduced condition numbers, CG iterations, and solve times, giving 1.1–4.7× speedups over diagonal/Jacobi preconditioning and 1.5–3× faster time-to-solution in mixed Poisson and biharmonic block solves.
watch_item: Performance depends on assembling and applying local element inverses efficiently, so benefits may be smaller if the element-local cost or implementation overhead is high.
paper_title: Broken-space Additive Schwarz Mass Inverse Approximations and (Block) Preconditioning
